title: Ipomoea trichocalyx
intro: Ipomoea trichocalyx is a plant species in the Convolvulaceae family, commonly known as a type of morning glory. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea trichocalyx include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ipomoea trichocalyx is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma.
